section_title: Contingent agreements to do or not to do anything, if an impossible event
content:
36. Contingent agreements to do or not to do anything, if an impossible event
contingent
happens, are void, whether the impossibility of the event is known or not to
on
impossible the parties to the agreement at the time when it is made.
events void
Illustrations
(a) A agrees to pay B 1,000 Taka if two straight lines should enclose a space.
The agreement is void.
(b) A agrees to pay B 1,000 Taka if B will marry A's daughter C. C was dead
at the time of the agreement. The agreement is void.
CHAPTER IV
OF THE PERFORMANCE OF CONTRACTS
Contracts which must be performed

Obligation
